title: Feature Pipeline Workspace Config
description: Generated by /init. Lives at ~/.claude/{workspace-slug}-config.json. Never committed to any repo (contains absolute paths).

slug.description: Kebab-case slug used in file paths and pipeline dirs

pipeline_dir.description: DEPRECATED — no longer read by any skill or script. Superseded by {workspace_root}/{slug}/runs/deliver/ (see /deliver pre-flight). Still accepted on existing configs for back-compat, but /discover no longer emits it; safe to delete.

memory.description: Opt-in GitHub-backed workspace memory. Absent ⇒ feature off (workspace dir stays local-only, current default). When enabled, the durable docs are committed (redacted) + pushed to a PRIVATE repo at the learning checkpoints. See docs/design/github-memory.md.

enabled.description: Master switch. When true, /discover bootstraps the repo and the learning skills sync via scripts/sync-memory.js.

remote.description: Git remote URL of the private memory repo (set at bootstrap, e.g. git@github.com:org/{slug}-memory.git).

repo_name.description: Repo name to create at bootstrap if no remote is given (default: {slug}-memory).

visibility.description: MUST be private — the memory may reference secrets even after redaction. Bootstrap refuses any other value.

sync_mode.description: How sync-memory.js publishes. 'commit' (default, solo): pull --rebase then commit straight to main. 'pr': always push a memory/<checkpoint> branch + open a PR for review. 'hybrid' (recommended for team-shared repos): structural changes (context/platform.md or context/adrs/**) open a PR; bookkeeping (audit-findings, observability, learn-log, config.portable) commits directly. A rebase conflict in any mode falls back to PR. See docs/design/github-memory.md.

repos.description: Map of repo short-name → repo config. Each key is a unique identifier used internally.

path.description: Absolute path to the repo root on disk

type.description: Tech stack type — drives agent selection

description.description: One-line description of what this repo does

spec_file.description: Path to the OpenAPI spec file relative to repo root (for api-service repos)

spec_copies.description: Map of service-name → relative path where this repo keeps a local copy of that service's spec. Repo-agnostic: any consuming repo (frontend, mock-server, api-service generating a typed client, infrastructure with API Gateway integration, doc site, contract-test repo, SDK) can declare entries. /deliver Phase 4 copies the source spec to each declared path whenever Phase 3b edits the source. Keys must match service names in the top-level services map.

services.description: Map of service short-name → service config. Services are the logical API boundaries the pipeline operates on.

repo.description: Key in the repos map that holds this service

spec_file.description: Spec file path relative to the repo root (overrides repo-level spec_file)

domain.description: Domain knowledge block. Optional — enriched by /init Phase B2.
